scenarios.title: Successful flat rate margin calculation with multiple sub-margin groups
scenarios.intent: Verify that flat rate margin is correctly calculated following the three-step process: aggregating positions by sub-margin group, applying flat margin rates, and applying the margin multiplier
given: A clearing participant has positions across multiple sub-margin groups
Sub-group 1 has long positions with total absolute market value of 30,000,000 HKD and short positions with total absolute market value of 60,000,000 HKD
Sub-group 2 has long positions with total absolute market value of 750,000 HKD and no short positions
Sub-group 3 has no long positions and short positions with total absolute market value of 300,000 HKD
Flat margin rates are defined under FieldType 3 (e.g., 12%, 30%, 55%)
A flat rate margin multiplier of 2 is assigned per DWH0081C report
when: The flat rate margin calculation is performed
then: The system includes only short positions for sub-group 1 (higher absolute market value)
The system includes only long positions for sub-group 2 (higher absolute market value)
The system includes only short positions for sub-group 3 (higher absolute market value)
assumptions: Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are retrieved from HKEX website configuration
Margin multiplier is obtained from Daily Participant Margin Multiplier Report (DWH0081C)

evidence.quote: Step 1: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market value of short positions separately
the total absolute short market value is higher than the total absolute long market value. Therefore, all short sub-group 1 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation
Step 2: Sum the product of absolute position market value and the flat margin rate under FieldType 3
Step 3: Apply flat rate margin multiplier by referring to the Daily Participant Margin Multiplier Report (DWH0081C)
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin calculation when long and short absolute market values are equal
scenarios.intent: Verify the system behavior when the total absolute market value of long positions equals the total absolute market value of short positions within a sub-margin group
given: A clearing participant has positions in a sub-margin group
The total absolute market value of long positions equals the total absolute market value of short positions (e.g., both are 50,000,000 HKD)
Flat margin rates are defined under FieldType 3
A flat rate margin multiplier is assigned per DWH0081C report
when: The flat rate margin calculation is performed for this sub-margin group
then: The system determines which positions to include based on the tie-breaking rule when long and short absolute market values are equal
The flat rate margin calculation proceeds with the determined position set
assumptions: The tie-breaking behavior when long and short absolute market values are equal is not explicitly defined in the provided evidence
The system may have a defined rule (e.g., include both, include neither, or prefer one side) that should be verified against HKEX specifications

evidence.quote: compare the total absolute market value of long positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group
the total absolute short market value is higher than the total absolute long market value. Therefore, all short sub-group 1 positions will be included

scenarios.title: Validation of required input data for flat rate margin calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify that the system validates all required input fields before performing the flat rate margin calculation
given: A clearing participant submits position data for flat rate margin calculation
Position data includes instrument IDs, quantities, and market values
when: The submitted position data is missing required fields such as absolute market value, position direction (long/short), or sub-margin group classification
then: The system rejects the calculation request
An appropriate validation error is returned indicating the missing or invalid required fields
assumptions: Required fields include: instrument ID, quantity, absolute market value in HKD equivalent, position direction (long/short), and sub-margin group assignment
Validation occurs before Step 1 of the flat rate margin calculation

evidence.quote: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market value of short positions separately for each position identified as per instructions in §3.2.2
compare the total absolute market value of long positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group of flat rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website

scenarios.title: Calculate and round aggregated market-risk-component margin
scenarios.intent: Verify the correct two-step calculation and rounding of aggregated market-risk-component margin per the specified formula and rounding parameter
given: A clearing participant has market risk components with Portfolio margin = 10,000,000
Flat rate margin = 15,180,000
Liquidation risk add-on = 266,865
Structured product add-on = 550,000
Corporate action position margin = 2,500,000
Holiday add-on = 18,433,039
The Initial Margin Risk Parameter File specifies a rounding parameter of 10,000
when: The system calculates the aggregated market-risk-component margin and applies rounding
then: The aggregated margin is calculated as 46,929,904
The aggregated margin is rounded up to 46,930,000

evidence.quote: Step 1: Calculate aggregated margin derived from market risk components
Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
Step 2: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter
46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin at exact rounding boundary
scenarios.intent: Verify rounding behavior when aggregated margin is exactly divisible by the rounding parameter of 10,000
given: A clearing participant has market risk components that sum to exactly 46,930,000
The Initial Margin Risk Parameter File specifies a rounding parameter of 10,000
when: The system rounds up the aggregated margin to the nearest 10,000
then: The rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin remains 46,930,000
assumptions: When the aggregated margin is exactly divisible by the rounding parameter, no upward adjustment is needed since the value is already at a rounding boundary

evidence.quote: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
e.g., 10,000 in the sample Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

scenarios.title: Validate all market risk components included in aggregation formula
scenarios.intent: Verify that all six market risk components are correctly summed and the rounding parameter from the Risk Parameter File is applied
given: A clearing participant has market risk components with values for Portfolio margin, Flat rate margin, Liquidation risk add-on, Structured product add-on, Corporate action position margin, and Holiday add-on
The Initial Margin Risk Parameter File contains a rounding parameter
when: The system calculates the aggregated market-risk-component margin
then: All six components are summed in the correct formula order
The rounding parameter from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is retrieved and applied to round up the aggregated margin

evidence.quote: Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components
Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

scenarios.title: Calculate Favorable MTM using portfolio market and contract values
scenarios.intent: Verify the standard calculation of Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) using the defined formula with valid input values
given: A clearing participant has a portfolio with market value of -300,550,000 HKD
The portfolio has a contract value of -287,850,000 HKD
when: The system calculates Favorable MTM using the formula: Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: The Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) is calculated as -12,700,000 HKD
The negative result is identified as an MTM requirement
The favorable MTM is set to zero since the result is negative
assumptions: Values are provided in HKD equivalent as per Appendix 4.6 for multi-currency portfolios
Numbers are rounded off at position level

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added after applying margin credit

scenarios.title: Favorable MTM calculation at zero result boundary
scenarios.intent: Verify the boundary condition where Market valuePortfolio equals Contract valuePortfolio, resulting in zero and testing the transition between favorable MTM and MTM requirement
given: A clearing participant has a portfolio with market value of -250,000,000 HKD
The portfolio has a contract value of -250,000,000 HKD
when: The system calculates Favorable MTM using the formula: Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: The Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) is calculated as 0 HKD
The result at the boundary is validated as neither favorable MTM nor MTM requirement
assumptions: A zero result represents the exact boundary between favorable MTM (positive) and MTM requirement (negative)
The system correctly handles the mutually exclusive nature of favorable MTM and MTM requirement at this boundary

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive

scenarios.title: Validate required input fields for Favorable MTM calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify that the system validates the presence and validity of required input data (Market valuePortfolio and Contract valuePortfolio) before performing the Favorable MTM calculation
given: A clearing participant submits a portfolio for initial margin calculation
The portfolio is missing the Market valuePortfolio data field
when: The system attempts to calculate Favorable MTM
then: The calculation is rejected with a validation error
An error message indicates that Market valuePortfolio is a required field for Favorable MTM calculation
assumptions: The system performs input validation before executing the calculation formula
Both Market valuePortfolio and Contract valuePortfolio are mandatory fields

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
The aggregated value of HKD equivalent contract value and HKD equivalent market value

scenarios.title: Calculate net margin after credit with margin exceeding credit
scenarios.intent: Verify that net margin after credit is correctly calculated when net margin exceeds the margin credit amount
given: A clearing participant has a net margin of 46,930,000
A margin credit of 5,000,000 is granted to the clearing participant
when: The margin credit is applied for margin calculation
then: The net margin after credit is calculated as 41,930,000

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Calculate net margin after credit when margin equals credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the floor boundary condition where net margin equals margin credit, resulting in zero net margin after credit
given: A clearing participant has a net margin of 5,000,000
A margin credit of 5,000,000 is granted to the clearing participant
when: The margin credit is applied for margin calculation
then: The net margin after credit is calculated as 0
assumptions: The Maximum[0, (Net margin - Margin credit)] formula ensures the result cannot be negative

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Validate margin credit value applied to clearing participant
scenarios.intent: Verify that the standard margin credit value is correctly assigned and validated for each clearing participant
given: A clearing participant is registered in the system
when: The margin credit is applied for margin calculation
then: The margin credit value is validated as 5,000,000
The margin credit is granted to the clearing participant
assumptions: The margin credit value of 5,000,000 is the normal standard value as specified in the guide

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP

feature: Total MTM and Margin Requirement Calculation

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement with all components
scenarios.intent: Verify that total MTM and margin requirement is correctly derived by summing net margin after credit and all other risk components
given: Net margin after credit value is available (e.g., 41,930,000)
MTM requirement value is available (e.g., 12,700,000)
Position limit add-on value is available (e.g., 487,332)
Credit risk add-on value is available (e.g., 12,000,000)
Ad-hoc add-on value is available (e.g., 600,000)
when: The system derives total MTM and margin requirement
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals the sum of all components (e.g., 67,717,332)
assumptions: All component values are valid and within acceptable ranges
Credit risk add-on is applicable (pre-VaR Platform launch)

evidence.quote: Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement when credit risk add-on is not applicable
scenarios.intent: Verify that total MTM and margin requirement is correctly calculated when credit risk add-on is zero due to VaR Platform launch
given: VaR Platform has been launched
Credit risk add-on is not applicable (value is 0)
All other risk components have valid values
when: The system derives total MTM and margin requirement
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals the sum of all applicable components excluding credit risk add-on
assumptions: HKSCC has notified CPs of VaR Platform launch as per documentation
Other component values remain valid and non-zero

evidence.quote: credit risk add-on will not be applicable upon the launch of VaR Platform
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on

scenarios.title: Validate margin requirement calculation with missing component values
scenarios.intent: Verify that the system validates input data and rejects calculation when required component values are missing or invalid
given: One or more margin component values are missing or null
The calculation request is submitted for total MTM and margin requirement
when: The system attempts to derive total MTM and margin requirement
then: The calculation is rejected
An appropriate validation error is returned indicating missing or invalid component data
assumptions: The system enforces data validation before performing calculations
All components listed in the formula are required inputs

evidence.quote: total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin after credit to other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on
